_cite.reason: URL-normalization 1c: strip trailing markdown-emphasis `*`/backtick glued to citation URLs in intelligence_item_sections.content_md (customer-checkable link + validate_item_provenance criterion-2 exact-compare). Operator ruling 2026-07-04. Same transform as the write-site fix.

prior.content_md:
- **MONITORING** — Monitor the Climate Change Authority's active Fifth Review of the Carbon Credits (Carbon Farming Initiative) Act 2011 (ACCU Scheme), now underway as of early 2026. The review cycle runs every three years under the Climate Change Authority Act 2011; outcomes may alter the carbon-credit instruments the workspace's Australian-based clients use to offset freight emissions, including Safeguard Mechanism-liable shippers. No hard registration deadline for observers, but public consultation windows close without notice. Action: nominate a regulatory watch lead to track CCA publications at climatechangeauthority.gov.au. *Source: ACCU Scheme Reviews, Climate Change Authority, March 2026. https://www.climatechangeauthority.gov.au/accu-scheme-reviews*

- **MONITORING** — Review the CCA's 2025 Annual Progress Report (tabled 2025), which found Australia is accelerating emissions reduction but that more action is needed. The report's sector-by-sector decarbonisation lens includes transport and logistics. Future annual progress reports can trigger Safeguard Mechanism rule amendments that would directly affect entities for whom the workspace arranges freight. Action: read the full report; flag any transport sector recommendations to commercial and sustainability leads. *Source: Annual Progress Report, Climate Change Authority, 2025. https://www.climatechangeauthority.gov.au/annual-progress-advice-0*

- **COMPETITIVE EDGE** — The CCA has published 2035 Targets Advice recommending an emissions reduction target of 62–70% below 2005 levels. This trajectory will be Australia's next NDC submission to the UNFCCC and is likely to intensify the Safeguard Mechanism's coverage and tighten carbon budgets across the supply chain. Action: brief leadership on the proposed target range and initiate scenario modelling for how tighter caps could affect surcharge structures and ACCU procurement for Australian air and road freight corridors. *Source: Climate Change Authority Homepage, Climate Change Authority, 2025. https://www.climatechangeauthority.gov.au/*

- **WINDOW CLOSING** — Public submissions for the Fifth ACCU Scheme Review are open now; the CCA is actively seeking stakeholder input on how ACCUs function in a decarbonising economy and broader ACCU market design. Freight operators whose clients hold carbon abatement contracts or voluntary offset positions have direct exposure to any scheme redesign. Action: determine whether the workspace or its clients wish to submit; confirm consultation closing date via the CCA's website immediately. *Source: ACCU Scheme Reviews, Climate Change Authority, March 2026. https://www.climatechangeauthority.gov.au/accu-scheme-reviews*
